title: Executive Order 11152—Establishing the President's Committee on Manpower

flags.justification: A broad, multi-agency committee (twelve-plus named agencies) with purely advisory and coordination functions.
Implements a specific statutory requirement (the Manpower Development and Training Act's annual presidential report to Congress), a real, statute-anchored accountability structure.
Multiple related functions all coherently serve one purpose.
A coordination and advisory body with no binding compliance regime.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A standing coordination body implementing a permanent statute, evergreen by nature.
Sec. 4 explicitly states nothing in the order shall abrogate, modify, or restrict any function vested by law in any federal agency — a genuinely protective non-abrogation clause.
A coordination body with no third-party compliance regime.
Implements the Manpower Development and Training Act of 1962, a significant Great Society-era workforce-development statute.
Broad, inclusive membership combined with the explicit non-abrogation clause avoids conflict with existing agency mandates.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
